skill.name: api-spectral
skill.description: API specification linting and security validation using Stoplight Spectral for OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, and Arazzo files. It helps review API designs against OWASP API Security Top 10 guidance, authentication rules, data exposure checks, and custom governance standards.
skill.summary: Lint API specifications for security, governance, and OWASP API risks with Spectral.
skill.author: AgentSecOps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, script, and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found these are mostly documentation examples, Spectral rules, and CI templates for security scanning, not hidden runtime behavior. One CI template includes a remote script piped to bash, so publication is acceptable only with a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell in CI Template
medium_findings.description: The CI template installs tfsec by piping a remote GitHub script directly to bash. This is not executed by the skill itself, but users who copy the template could run unpinned remote code in CI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line directly uses curl with a pipe to bash, which is a recognized supply-chain risk. Confidence is high for the pattern, but impact is reduced because it is an optional template rather than automatic skill execution.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Mostly Security Examples
low_findings.description: Many flagged commands, weak cryptography terms, XSS sinks, secret names, and reconnaissance words appear in examples that teach users how to detect insecure API patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text labels the snippets as vulnerable examples or rule mappings. This strongly supports a false-positive interpretation for most pattern matches.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands for Spectral Workflows
low_findings.description: The numerous shell-command detections are command snippets for installing Spectral, linting API specifications, and wiring CI checks. They do not show covert command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are fenced examples and workflow documentation. No evidence found that the skill silently runs these commands outside user-directed workflows.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: A targeted review for override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, and requests to skip analysis found no evidence in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The search covered the skill files for the injection phrases specified by the audit task. Confidence is not perfect because semantic variants may exist, but no evidence was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: A CI template pipes a remote installer script into bash. Prefer pinned releases, checksum verification, or package-manager installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present and would execute remote code if copied into CI. It is isolated to a template, so it is a warning rather than a publication blocker.

content.value_statement: API specifications can miss security requirements before implementation starts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Spectral linting, OWASP API checks, and governance workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides Spectral setup for OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, and Arazzo specifications.
Provides OWASP API Security Top 10 rule mappings for API design review.
Includes reusable Spectral rules for authentication, HTTPS, PII, and schema controls.
Shows CI templates for GitHub Actions and broader security scanning workflows.
Explains custom rule authoring, testing, and false positive review practices.
limitations: It is guidance and templates, not a bundled executable scanner.
Referenced helper scripts are documented but not present in this package.
Spectral results need human review before security sign-off.
CI templates must be adapted and hardened before production use.

use_cases.title: Review API Designs Before Build
use_cases.description: Check OpenAPI or AsyncAPI files for missing authentication, insecure servers, sensitive query parameters, and OWASP API gaps.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Governance
use_cases.description: Create shared Spectral rulesets that enforce organization standards across many API repositories.

use_cases.title: Add API Checks to CI
use_cases.description: Use the workflow templates to report Spectral findings during pull requests and release pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Lint One API Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of one OpenAPI file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to help me lint my OpenAPI file with Spectral. Explain the setup, command, and how to read the findings.

prompt_templates.title: Map Findings to OWASP API
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn Spectral output into security review notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to map these Spectral findings to OWASP API Security Top 10 categories and suggest practical remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create Custom Ruleset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to draft a Spectral ruleset for our API standards. Include authentication, HTTPS, pagination, and sensitive data checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI Enforcement Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to design a phased CI rollout for API specification linting across many repositories. Include thresholds, exceptions, reports, and developer feedback.

output_examples.input: An OpenAPI file has unsecured write operations and HTTP server URLs.
output: A prioritized finding list grouped by authentication, transport security, and OWASP API category.
Remediation guidance that adds security requirements and replaces HTTP servers with HTTPS URLs.
A suggested Spectral command and severity threshold for verification.

output_examples.input: A team wants API rules in pull requests.
output: A CI workflow outline that installs Spectral, runs the selected ruleset, stores reports, and comments on pull requests.
A warning to pin downloaded dependencies and avoid remote shell installers.
A rollout plan that starts with warnings before enforcing errors.

output_examples.input: A security team needs a custom rule for sensitive query parameters.
output: A rule design that targets query parameter names linked to secrets or personal data.
A severity recommendation and OWASP API mapping.
Testing guidance with valid and invalid API specification examples.

best_practices: Start with built-in Spectral rules, then add custom security rules gradually.
Review findings manually before treating them as confirmed vulnerabilities.
Pin CI dependencies and verify downloaded tools before running them.
anti_patterns: Do not copy CI templates into production without reviewing remote downloads.
Do not include real secrets, tokens, or private URLs in API examples.
Do not fail builds on new rules before teams have reviewed expected false positives.

faq.question: Does this skill run Spectral automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, rulesets, and templates that help an AI assistant guide Spectral usage.

faq.question: Which API formats are covered?
faq.answer: The skill discusses OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, and Arazzo, with most examples focused on OpenAPI and Spectral.

faq.question: Can it prove an API is secure?
faq.answer: No. Spectral checks the specification. Runtime authorization, implementation, and business logic still need separate review.

faq.question: Why did the audit find many risky patterns?
faq.answer: Most patterns are intentionally vulnerable examples, rule names, or CI snippets used for security education and detection.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: One CI template pipes a remote installer script to bash. Replace it with a pinned and verified installation method.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
